edition.dagbeeld: Iran rouwt om Khamenei terwijl de wereld zijn adem inhoudt over de opvolging; in Amerika overschaduwt Trumps partijdige toespraak het 250-jarig jubileum; en dichter bij huis zet het Farage-schandaal over niet-aangegeven giften de Britse politiek op scherp. Natuur en klimaat drukken hun stempel op Europa met nieuwe grote bosbranden.

article.title: Nigel Farage did not declare gifts from crypto entrepreneur convicted of fraud
article.summary: Reform UK’s Robert Jenrick says Farage accepted staff, security and accommodation from George Cottrell before becoming an MP

opening.note: Farage verzweeg giften van veroordeelde fraudeur — partijgenoot Jenrick blaast de zaak op.
opening.label: scoop

article.title: Mourners Chant ‘Revenge’ at Funeral Prayers for Iran’s Slain Supreme Leader
article.summary: On the second day of public mourning for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in February, there was still no sign of his son and successor, Mojtaba Khamenei.

front.note: Rouwmenigte eist wraak; opvolger Mojtaba blijft ongrijpbaar aanwezig.
front.label: reportage

article.title: How Trump is turning NATO into a cash machine
article.summary: The U.S. president has refashioned the alliance into something far different from its original intention.

front.note: Trump hervormt NAVO stilletjes van veiligheidsalliantie tot geldmachine.

article.title: Ukraine striking Russian energy infrastructure at unprecedented rate

article.title: Heatwave: Fires devastate forests across Europe
article.summary: Hundreds of firefighters battled infernos that burnt thousands of hectares in France, Spain and Portugal, three countries where, in some places, temperatures were predicted to reach 40ºC on Sunday.

front.note: Bosbranden teisteren Spanje, Portugal en Frankrijk bij extreme hitte.
front.label: breaking

article.summary: President Tamás Sulyok has vowed not to leave office. His term ends in 2029.

items.title: Peruvian and Colombian presidential elections: Expatriate vote helps far right clinch victory
items.summary: Peruvians living abroad helped Keiko Fujimori to win the presidency, even though only a minority of Peru's residents voted for her. Expatriate Colombians also massively backed Abelardo de la Espriella, though this was not as decisive.
